issues.title: Drawings page lacks ARIA labels and keyboard navigation on drawing cards
issues.description: Drawing cards/rows have no aria-label, role attributes, or keyboard event handlers. Users cannot navigate drawings via keyboard or screen reader. The grid/list toggle and discipline filter pills also lack aria attributes. This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards for accessibility.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the main Drawings component: Add role='list' to the drawings container and role='listitem' with aria-label={`Drawing ${d.setNumber} ${d.disciplineLabel} ${d.title || ''}`} to each drawing card. Add tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler (Enter opens detail) to each card. Add aria-label to discipline filter buttons (e.g. aria-label={`Filter by ${discipline}`}) and aria-pressed={isActive} for toggle state. Add aria-label='Toggle grid view' and aria-label='Toggle list view' to view toggle buttons. BEFORE: Drawing cards are div elements with no ARIA attributes, no keyboard handlers, filters have no pressed state. AFTER: All interactive elements have proper ARIA labels, keyboard navigation works with Tab/Enter, filter pills announce pressed state to screen readers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Files page missing empty state for no documents uploaded
issues.description: When a project has no files, the Files page shows a blank content area or a generic message instead of a contextual empty state with illustration, specific message ('No files uploaded yet'), and a primary CTA ('Upload Files'). This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for Drawings page criterion 10 and Global empty state standards.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Files.tsx, in the main Files component: Find where the file list is rendered (likely mapping over files array). BEFORE: When files array is empty, either nothing renders or a generic 'No data' message appears. AFTER: When files array is empty AND not loading, render the EmptyState component from src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx with icon='FileText', title='No files uploaded yet', description='Upload project documents, drawings, and photos to keep everything organized in one place.', actionLabel='Upload Files', and onAction that triggers the upload flow. Import EmptyState from '../components/ui/EmptyState'. Ensure the empty state only shows when isLoading is false and data length is 0. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Drawing viewer has no mobile responsive layout or touch gesture support
issues.description: The DrawingViewer component uses fixed pixel widths and does not adapt to mobile viewports. There is no pinch-to-zoom, no collapsible toolbar, and markup tools are not touch-friendly (targets below 44px). PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Drawings criterion 13 requires pinch-to-zoom, collapsible bottom toolbar, and quick-link to create RFI from markup on mobile.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, in the DrawingViewer component: BEFORE: The container uses fixed width/height values and the toolbar is always visible at top with small button targets. AFTER: 1) Change the outer container to use width:'100%', height:'100%', position:'relative' so it fills its parent responsively. 2) Add a media query check using the useMediaQuery hook (import from '../../hooks/useMediaQuery'): const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)'). 3) When isMobile is true, move the toolbar to the bottom with position:'fixed', bottom:0, left:0, right:0, zIndex:50, and add a collapse toggle button. 4) Set all toolbar button minWidth and minHeight to 44 for touch targets. 5) Add touch-action:'manipulation' to the canvas/viewer container style to enable native pinch-to-zoom. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Document upload has no error feedback or retry mechanism
issues.description: When a file upload fails (network error, size limit, invalid type), there is no visible error toast, inline error message, or retry button. The upload silently fails or shows a console error. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require toast notifications on API failures and inline retry buttons for network failures.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx, in the UploadZone component: BEFORE: Upload errors are caught but not surfaced to the user. Failed uploads show no visual feedback. AFTER: 1) In the upload error catch block, add a visible error state: set a local state variable like setUploadError({fileName: file.name, message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Upload failed'}). 2) Render the error inline below the drop zone: when uploadError is set, show a div with style={{padding: 12, background: '#FEF2F2', border: '1px solid #FECACA', borderRadius: 8, marginTop: 8, display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'space-between'}} containing the error message text and a Retry button (onClick re-attempts the upload with the same file). 3) Add aria-live='polite' to the error container so screen readers announce it. 4) Clear the error state on successful upload or when user dismisses. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Drawings page missing revision history timeline and version comparison
issues.description: The Drawings page does not expose revision history or version comparison UI despite the backend supporting drawing_revisions table and getDrawingRevisionHistory API. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Drawings criterion 2 requires revision management with auto-increment, previous revision access, and 'View Previous Revisions' dropdown. Criterion 12 requires side-by-side comparison with opacity slider.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the drawing detail view or drawer section: BEFORE: When a drawing is selected, only the current version is shown with no revision history or comparison controls. AFTER: 1) Import getDrawingRevisionHistory from '../../api/endpoints/documents'. 2) When a drawing is selected (detail panel opens), fetch its revision history using getDrawingRevisionHistory(selectedDrawing.id). 3) Render a 'Revision History' section in the detail panel showing a vertical timeline of revisions: each entry displays revision_number, issued_date, issued_by, and change_description. Style each entry with a left border line (borderLeft: '2px solid #E5E7EB', paddingLeft: 16, marginBottom: 12). The current (non-superseded) revision gets a green dot indicator; superseded ones get gray dots. 4) Add a 'Current' badge (using the Tag component from Primitives) next to the active revision. 5) Each revision entry should be clickable to view that version. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore offers a full plan room with version comparison, markup persistence, and RFI linking from drawings. Fieldwire excels at task-to-plan linking with Apple Pencil support. SiteSync has the backend schema (drawings, drawing_revisions, drawing_markups) but the frontend is skeletal: no revision UI, no working markup persistence, no mobile drawing viewer, and no version comparison. The document management module is roughly at 30% of what Procore ships. Autodesk Construction Cloud leads with BIM integration and automated clash detection. SiteSync's AI conflict detection concept is differentiated but not yet surfaced in the drawings UI.
summary: The Document Management module has decent backend infrastructure (drawings table, revisions, markups, files with folder hierarchy) but the frontend is severely underdeveloped. The Drawings page lacks revision history exposure, version comparison, mobile-responsive viewing, and accessible navigation. The Files page is missing proper empty states and upload error handling. Drawing markup tools exist in component form but are not connected to a mobile-friendly experience with proper touch targets. The module scores lowest in mobile-first (18) and accessibility (15) because interactive elements have no ARIA attributes and the drawing viewer does not adapt to mobile viewports. The five surgical fixes target the most impactful gaps: accessibility compliance, empty states, mobile responsiveness, error handling, and domain-critical revision history. These fixes bring the module from 28 to an estimated 40-45 without adding new features.
